title: Debt Ratios: A firm has total assets of 1000000 and total liabilities...
summary: Calculate equity value by subtracting total liabilities from total assets.
prompt: A firm has total assets of $1,000,000 and total liabilities of $600,000. What is the equity value?
solution: Equity Value = Total Assets - Total Liabilities = $1,000,000 - $600,000 = $400,000. This calculation is fundamental for determining the net worth of the firm.
